pr-evidence/legacy-storage-job-defaults/.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewer Banach initially found import-order holes for in-place public class mutations and a brittle .pytest_cache test path. The patch added generic class surface fingerprinting, tmp_path-based subprocess output, an in-place __init__/__setattr__ regression, reran the verification gate, and Banach approved with no remaining merge blockers.
verification: python -m py_compile src/restaurant_kv_serving/databricks_storage_benchmark_job.py tests/test_databricks_storage_benchmark_job.py; poetry run pytest tests/test_databricks_storage_benchmark_job.py::test_legacy_storage_benchmark_job_import_order_does_not_capture_public_monkeypatch tests/test_databricks_storage_benchmark_job.py::test_legacy_storage_benchmark_job_import_order_ignores_in_place_public_class_mutation -q; poetry run pytest tests/test_databricks_storage_benchmark_job.py -q; poetry run pytest tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q; poetry run pytest -q; git diff --check; poetry check; poetry build; secret scan: rg only matched existing fake credential detector fixtures/evidence text
what_changed: Isolated the legacy Databricks storage-benchmark job facade from pre-import monkeypatches of document_kv_cache.databricks_storage_benchmark_job, including patched constants, replaced config classes, in-place config class mutations, and runner writer hooks.
pr-evidence/legacy-storage-job-defaults/.why: The migration-only restaurant_kv_serving facade must preserve old compatibility hooks without letting public document namespace test hooks or import-order mutations become legacy defaults. This keeps the V1 storage benchmark Databricks evidence path stable while the implementation moves to document_kv_cache.
